django.conf import settings\n\n__all__ = [\n    'utc', 'get_fixed_timezone',\n    'get_default_timezone', 'get_default_timezone_name',\n    'get_current_timezone', 'get_current_timezone_name',\n pytz.utc\n\n_PYTZ_BASE_CLASSES = (pytz.tzinfo.BaseTzInfo, pytz._FixedOffset)\n# In releases prior to 2018.4, pytz.UTC was not a subclass of BaseTzInfo\nif not isinstance(pytz.UTC, pytz._FixedOffset):\n    _PYTZ_BASE_CLASSES = _PYTZ_BASE_CLASSES + (type(pytz.UTC),)\n\n\ndef get_fixed_timezone(offset):\n    \"\"\"Return a tzinfo instance with a fixed offset from UTC.\"\"\"\n    if isinstance(offset, timedelta):\n        offset = offset.total_seconds() // 60\n    sign = '-' if offset < 0 else '+'\n    hhmm = '%02d%02d' % divmod(abs(offset), 60)\n    name = sign + hhmm\n    return timezone(timedelta(minutes=offset), name)\n\n\n# In order to avoid accessing settings at compile time,\n# wrap the logic in a function and cache the result.\n@functools.lru_cache()\ndef get_default_timezone():\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the default time zone as a tzinfo instance.\n\n    This is the time zone defined by settings.TIME_ZONE.\n    \"\"\"\n    return pytz.timezone(settings.TIME_ZONE)\n\n\n# This function exists for consistency with get_current_timezone_name\ndef get_default_timezone_name():\n    \"\"\"Return the name of the default time zone.\"\"\"\n    return _get_timezone_name(get_default_timezone())\n\n\n_active = Local()\n\n\ndef get_current_timezone():\n    \"\"\"Return the currently active time zone as a tzinfo instance.\"\"\"\n    return getattr(_active, \"value\", get_default_timezone())\n\n\ndef get_current_timezone_name():\n    \"\"\"Return the name of the currently active time zone.\"\"\"\n    return _get_timezone_name(get_current_timezone())\n\n\ndef _get_timezone_name(timezone):\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the offset for fixed offset timezones, or the name of timezone if\n    not set.\n    \"\"\"\n    return timezone.tzname(None) or str(timezone)\n\n# Timezone selection functions.\n\n# These functions don't change os.environ['TZ'] and call time.tzset()\n# because it isn't thread safe.\n\n\ndef activate(timezone):\n    \"\"\"\n    Set the time zone for the current thread.\n\n    The ``timezone`` argument must be an instance of a tzinfo subclass or a\n    time zone name.\n    \"\"\"\n    if isinstance(timezone, tzinfo):\n        _active.value = timezone\n    elif isinstance(timezone, str):\n        _active.value = pytz.timezone(timezone)\n    else:\n        raise ValueError(\"Invalid timezone: %r\" % timezone)\n\n\ndef deactivate():\n    \"\"\"\n    Unset the time zone for the current thread.\n\n    Django will then use the time zone defined by settings.TIME_ZONE.\n    \"\"\"\n    if hasattr(_active, \"value\"):\n        del _active.value\n\n\nclass override(ContextDecorator):\n    \"\"\"\n    Temporarily set the time zone for the current thread.\n\n    This is a context manager that uses django.utils.timezone.activate()\n    to set the timezone on entry and restores the previously active timezone\n    on exit.\n\n    The ``timezone`` argument must be an instance of a ``tzinfo`` subclass, a\n    time zone name, or ``None``. If it is ``None``, Django enables the default\n    time zone.\n    \"\"\"\n    def __init__(self, timezone):\n        self.timezone = timezone\n\n    def __enter__(self):\n        self.old_timezone = getattr(_active, 'value', None)\n        if self.timezone is None:\n            deactivate()\n        else:\n            activate(self.timezone)\n\n    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_value, traceback):\n        if self.old_timezone is None:\n            deactivate()\n        else:\n            _active.value = self.old_timezone\n\n\n# Templates\n\ndef template_localtime(value, use_tz=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Check if value is a datetime and converts it to local time if necessary.\n\n    If use_tz is provided and is not None, that will force the value to\n    be converted (or not), overriding the value of settings.USE_TZ.\n\n    This function is designed for use by the template engine.\n    \"\"\"\n    should_convert = (\n        isinstance(value, datetime) and\n        (settings.USE_TZ if use_tz is None else use_tz) and\n        not is_naive(value) and\n        getattr(value, 'convert_to_local_time', True)\n    )\n    return localtime(value) if should_convert else value\n\n\n# Utilities\n\ndef localtime(value=None, timezone=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert an aware datetime.datetime to local time.\n\n    Only aware datetimes are allowed. When value is omitted, it defaults to\n    now().\n\n    Local time is defined by the current time zone, unless another time zone\n    is specified.\n    \"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        value = now()\n    if timezone is None:\n        timezone = get_current_timezone()\n    # Emulate the behavior of astimezone() on Python < 3.6.\n    if is_naive(value):\n        raise ValueError(\"localtime() cannot be applied to a naive datetime\")\n    return value.astimezone(timezone)\n\n\ndef localdate(value=None, timezone=None):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert an aware datetime to local time and return the value's date.\n\n    Only aware datetimes are allowed. When value is omitted, it defaults to\n    now().\n\n    Local time is defined by the current time zone, unless another time zone is\n    specified.\n    \"\"\"\n    return localtime(value, timezone).date()\n\n\ndef now():\n    \"\"\"\n    Return an aware or naive datetime.datetime, depending on settings.USE_TZ.\n    \"\"\"\n    return datetime.now(tz=utc if settings.USE_TZ else None)\n\n\n# By design, these four functions don't perform any checks on their arguments.\n# The caller should ensure that they don't receive an invalid value like None.\n\ndef is_aware(value):\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a given datetime.datetime is aware.\n\n    The concept is defined in Python's docs:\n    https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.tzinfo\n\n    Assuming value.tzinfo is either None or a proper datetime.tzinfo,\n    value.utcoffset() implements the appropriate logic.\n    \"\"\"\n    return value.utcoffset() is not None\n\n\ndef is_naive(value):\n    \"\"\"\n    Determine if a given datetime.datetime is naive.\n\n    The concept is defined in Python's docs:\n    https://docs.python.org/library/datetime.html#datetime.tzinfo\n\n    Assuming value.tzinfo is either None or a proper datetime.tzinfo,\n    value.utcoffset() implements the appropriate logic.\n    \"\"\"\n    return value.utcoffset() is None\n\n\ndef make_aware(value, timezone=None, is_dst=None):\n    \"\"\"Make a naive datetime.datetime in a given time zone aware.\"\"\"\n    if timezone is None:\n        timezone = get_current_timezone()\n    if _is_pytz_zone(timezone):\n        # This method is available for pytz time zones.\n        return timezone.localize(value, is_dst=is_dst)\n    else:\n        # Check that we won't overwrite the timezone of an aware datetime.\n        if is_aware(value):\n            raise ValueError(\n                \"make_aware expects a naive datetime, got %s\" % value)\n        # This may be wrong around DST changes!\n        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone)\n\n\ndef make_naive(value, timezone=None):\n    \"\"\"Make an aware datetime.datetime naive in a given time zone.\"\"\"\n    if timezone is None:\n        timezone = get_current_timezone()\n    # Emulate the behavior of astimezone() on Python < 3.6.\n    if is_naive(value):\n        raise ValueError(\"make_naive() cannot be applied to a naive datetime\")\n    return value.astimezone(timezone).replace(tzinfo=None)\n\n\ndef _is_pytz_zone(tz):\n    \"\"\"Checks if a zone is a pytz zone.\"\"\"\n    return isinstance(tz, _PYTZ_BASE_CLASSES)\n\n\ndef _datetime_ambiguous_or_imaginary(dt, tz):\n    if _is_pytz_zone(tz):\n        try:\n            tz.utcoffset(dt)\n        except (pytz.AmbiguousTimeError, pytz.NonExistentTimeError):\n            return True\n        else:\n            return False\n\n    return tz.utcoffset(dt.replace(fold=not dt.fold)) != tz.utcoffset(dt)\n","message":"Command executed successfully.","display":[],"extras":null}}}}

Per PEP 249, by\n    63\t        # default, it isn't.\n    64\t        self.autocommit = False\n    65\t        # Tracks if the connection is in a transaction managed by 'atomic'.\n    66\t        self.in_atomic_block = False\n    67\t        # Increment to generate unique savepoint ids.\n    68\t        self.savepoint_state = 0\n    69\t        # List of savepoints created by 'atomic'.\n    70\t        self.savepoint_ids = []\n    71\t        # Tracks if the outermost 'atomic' block should commit on exit,\n    72\t        # ie. if autocommit was active on entry.\n    73\t        self.commit_on_exit = True\n    74\t        # Tracks if the transaction should be rolled back to the next\n    75\t        # available savepoint because of an exception in an inner block.\n    76\t        self.needs_rollback = False\n    77\t\n    78\t        # Connection termination related attributes.\n    79\t        self.close_at = None\n    80\t        self.closed_in_transaction = False\n    81\t        self.errors_occurred = False\n    82\t\n    83\t        # Thread-safety related attributes.\n    84\t        self._thread_sharing_lock = threading.Lock()\n    85\t        self._thread_sharing_count = 0\n    86\t        self._thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n    87\t\n    88\t        # A list of no-argument functions to run when the transaction commits.\n    89\t        # Each entry is an (sids, func) tuple, where sids is a set of the\n    90\t        # active savepoint IDs when this function was registered.\n    91\t        self.run_on_commit = []\n    92\t\n    93\t        # Should we run the on-commit hooks the next time set_autocommit(True)\n    94\t        # is called?\n    95\t        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = False\n    96\t\n    97\t        # A stack of wrappers to be invoked around execute()/executemany()\n    98\t        # calls. It's the function's responsibility to\n   100\t        # call execute(sql, params, many, context).\n   101\t        self.execute_wrappers = []\n   102\t\n   103\t        self.client = self.client_class(self)\n   104\t        self.creation = self.creation_class(self)\n   105\t        self.features = self.features_class(self)\n   106\t        self.introspection = self.introspection_class(self)\n   107\t        self.ops = self.ops_class(self)\n   108\t        self.validation = self.validation_class(self)\n   109\t\n   110\t    def ensure_timezone(self):\n   111\t        \"\"\"\n   112\t        Ensure the connection's timezone is set to `self.timezone_name` and\n   113\t        return whether it changed or not.\n   114\t        \"\"\"\n   115\t        return False\n   116\t\n   117\t    @cached_property\n   118\t    def timezone(self):\n   119\t        \"\"\"\n   120\t        Return a tzinfo of the database connection time zone.\n   121\t\n   122\t        This is only used when time zone support is enabled. When a datetime is\n   123\t        read from the database, it is always returned in this time zone.\n   124\t\n   125\t        When the database backend supports time zones, it doesn't matter which\n   126\t        time zone Django uses, as long as aware datetimes are used everywhere.\n   127\t        Other users connecting to the database can choose their own time zone.\n   128\t\n   129\t        When the database backend doesn't support time zones, the time zone\n   130\t        Django uses may be constrained by the requirements of other users of\n   131\t        the database.\n   132\t        \"\"\"\n   133\t        if not settings.USE_TZ:\n   134\t            return None\n   135\t        elif self.settings_dict['TIME_ZONE'] is None:\n   136\t            return timezone.utc\n   137\t        else:\n   138\t            return pytz.timezone(self.settings_dict['TIME_ZONE'])\n   139\t\n   140\t    @cached_property\n   141\t    def timezone_name(self):\n   142\t        \"\"\"\n   143\t        Name of the time zone of the database connection.\n   144\t        \"\"\"\n   145\t        if not settings.USE_TZ:\n   146\t            return settings.TIME_ZONE\n   147\t        elif self.settings_dict['TIME_ZONE'] is None:\n   148\t            return 'UTC'\n   149\t        else:\n   150\t            return self.settings_dict['TIME_ZONE']\n   151\t\n   152\t    @property\n   153\t    def queries_logged(self):\n   154\t        return self.force_debug_cursor or settings.DEBUG\n   155\t\n   156\t    @property\n   157\t    def queries(self):\n   158\t        if len(self.queries_log) == self.queries_log.maxlen:\n   159\t            warnings.warn(\n   160\t                \"Limit for query logging exceeded, only the last {} queries \"\n   161\t                \"will be returned.\".format(self.queries_log.maxlen))\n   162\t        return list(self.queries_log)\n   163\t\n   164\t    # ##### Backend-specific methods for creating connections and cursors #####\n   165\t\n   166\t    def get_connection_params(self):\n   167\t        \"\"\"Return a dict of parameters suitable for get_new_connection.\"\"\"\n   168\t        raise NotImplementedError('subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a get_connection_params() method')\n   169\t\n   170\t    def get_new_connection(self, conn_params):\n   171\t        \"\"\"Open a connection to the database.\"\"\"\n   172\t        raise NotImplementedError('subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require a get_new_connection() method')\n   173\t\n   174\t    def init_connection_state(self):\n   175\t        \"\"\"Initialize the database connection settings.\"\"\"\n   176\t        raise NotImplementedError('subclasses of BaseDatabaseWrapper may require an init_connection_state() method')\n   177\t\n   178\t    def create_cursor(self, name=None):\n   179\t        \"\"\"Create a cursor. Assume that the connection is closed.\"\"\"\n   187\t        # Check for invalid configurations.\n   188\t        self.check_settings()\n   189\t        # In case the previous connection was closed while in an atomic block\n   190\t        self.in_atomic_block = False\n   191\t        self.savepoint_ids = []\n   192\t        self.needs_rollback = False\n   193\t        # ##### Backend-specific wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n   222\t\n   223\t    def _prepare_cursor(self, cursor):\n   224\t        \"\"\"\n   225\t        Validate the connection is usable and perform database cursor wrapping.\n   226\t        \"\"\"\n   227\t        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   228\t        if self.queries_logged:\n   229\t            wrapped_cursor = self.make_debug_cursor(cursor)\n   230\t        else:\n   231\t            wrapped_cursor = self.make_cursor(cursor)\n   232\t        return wrapped_cursor\n   233\t\n   234\t    def _cursor(self, name=None):\n   235\t        self.ensure_connection()\n   236\t        with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   237\t            return self._prepare_cursor(self.create_cursor(name))\n   238\t\n   239\t    def _commit(self):\n   240\t        if self.connection is not None:\n   241\t            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   242\t                return self.connection.commit()\n   243\t\n   244\t    def _rollback(self):\n   245\t        if self.connection is not None:\n   246\t            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   247\t                return self.connection.rollback()\n   248\t\n   249\t    def _close(self):\n   250\t        if self.connection is not None:\n   251\t            with self.wrap_database_errors:\n   252\t                return self.connection.close()\n   253\t\n   254\t    # ##### Generic wrappers for PEP-249 connection methods #####\n   255\t\n   256\t    @async_unsafe\n   257\t    def cursor(self):\n   258\t        \"\"\"Create a cursor, opening a connection if necessary.\"\"\"\n   259\t        return self._cursor()\n   260\t\n   261\t    @async_unsafe\n   262\t    def commit(self):\n   263\t        \"\"\"Commit a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n   264\t        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   265\t        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   266\t        self._commit()\n   267\t        # A successful commit means that the database connection works.\n   268\t        self.errors_occurred = False\n   269\t        self.run_commit_hooks_on_set_autocommit_on = True\n   270\t\n   271\t    @async_unsafe\n   272\t    def rollback(self):\n   273\t        \"\"\"Roll back a transaction and reset the dirty flag.\"\"\"\n   274\t        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   275\t        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   276\t        self._rollback()\n   277\t        # A successful rollback means that the database connection works.\n   278\t        self.errors_occurred = False\n   279\t        self.needs_rollback = False\n   280\t        self.run_on_commit = []\n   281\t\n   282\t    @async_unsafe\n   283\t    def close(self):\n   284\t        \"\"\"Close the connection to the database.\"\"\"\n   285\t        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   286\t        self.run_on_commit = []\n   287\t\n   288\t        # Don't call validate_no_atomic_block() to avoid making it difficult\n   289\t        # to get rid of a connection in an invalid state. The next connect()\n   290\t        # will reset the transaction state anyway.\n   291\t        if self.closed_in_transaction or self.connection is None:\n   292\t            return\n   293\t        try:\n   294\t            self._close()\n   295\t        finally:\n   296\t            if self.in_atomic_block:\n   297\t                self.closed_in_transaction = True\n   298\t                self.needs_rollback = True\n   299\t            else:\n   300\t                self.connection = None\n   301\t\n   302\t    # ##### Backend-specific savepoint management methods #####\n   303\t\n   304\t    def _savepoint(self, sid):\n   305\t        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   306\t            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_create_sql(sid))\n   307\t\n   308\t    def _savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n   309\t        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   310\t            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_rollback_sql(sid))\n   311\t\n   312\t    def _savepoint_commit(self, sid):\n   313\t        with self.cursor() as cursor:\n   314\t            cursor.execute(self.ops.savepoint_commit_sql(sid))\n   315\t\n   316\t    def _savepoint_allowed(self):\n   317\t        # Savepoints cannot be created outside a transaction\n   318\t        return self.features.uses_savepoints and not self.get_autocommit()\n   319\t\n   320\t    # ##### Generic savepoint management methods #####\n   321\t\n   322\t    @async_unsafe\n   323\t    def savepoint(self):\n   324\t        \"\"\"\n   325\t        Create a savepoint inside the current transaction. Return an\n   326\t        identifier for the savepoint that will be used for the subsequent\n   327\t        rollback or commit. Do nothing if savepoints are not supported.\n   328\t        \"\"\"\n   329\t        if not self._savepoint_allowed():\n   330\t            return\n   331\t\n   332\t        thread_ident = _thread.get_ident()\n   333\t        tid = str(thread_ident).replace('-', '')\n   334\t\n   335\t        self.savepoint_state += 1\n   336\t        sid = \"s%s_x%d\" % (tid, self.savepoint_state)\n   337\t\n   338\t        self.validate_thread_sharing()\n   339\t        self._savepoint(sid)\n   340\t\n   341\t        return sid\n   342\t\n   343\t    @async_unsafe\n   344\t    def savepoint_rollback(self, sid):\n   345\t        \"\"\"\n   346\t        Roll back to a savepoint. To avoid this buggy behavior and to actually enter a new\n   399\t        transaction, an explicit BEGIN is required. Using\n   400\t        force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit=True will issue an\n   401\t        explicit BEGIN with SQLite. This option will be ignored for other\n   402\t        backends.\n   403\t        \"\"\"\n   404\t        self.validate_no_atomic_block()\n   405\t        self.ensure_connection()\n   406\t\n   407\t        start_transaction_under_autocommit = (\n   408\t            force_begin_transaction_with_broken_autocommit and not autocommit and\n   409\t            hasattr(self, '_start_transaction_under_autocommit')\n   410\t        )\n   411\t\n   412\t        if start_transaction_under_autocommit:\n   413\t            self._start_transaction_under_autocommit()\n   414\t        else:\n   415\t            self._set_autocommit(autocommit)\n   416\t\n   417\t        self.autocommit = autocommit\n   418\t\n   419\t        if autocommit and transaction. You can't \"\n   449\t                \"execute queries until the end of the 'atomic' block.\")\n   450\t\n   451\t    # ##### Foreign key constraints checks handling #####\n   452\t\n   453\t    @contextmanager\n   454\t    def constraint_checks_disabled(self):\n   455\t        \"\"\"\n   456\t        Disable foreign key constraint checking.\n   457\t        \"\"\"\n   458\t        disabled = self.disable_constraint_checking()\n   459\t        try:\n   460\t            yield\n   461\t        finally:\n   462\t            if disabled:\n   463\t                self.enable_constraint_checking()\n   464\t\n   465\t    def disable_constraint_checking(self):\n   466\t        \"\"\"\n   467\t        Backends can implement as needed to temporarily disable foreign key\n   468\t        constraint checking. If the application didn't restore the original autocommit setting,\n   509\t            # don't take chances, drop the connection.\n   510\t            if self.get_autocommit() != self.settings_dict['AUTOCOMMIT']:\n   511\t                self.close()\n   512\t                return\n   513\t\n   514\t            # If an exception other than DataError or IntegrityError occurred\n   515\t            # since the last commit / rollback, check if the connection works.\n   516\t            if self.errors_occurred:\n   517\t                if self.is_usable():\n   518\t                    self.errors_occurred = False\n   519\t                else:\n   520\t                    self.close()\n   521\t                    return\n   522\t\n   523\t            if self.close_at is not None and time.monotonic() >= self.close_at:\n   524\t                self.close()\n   525\t                return\n   526\t\n   527\t    # ##### Thread safety handling #####\n   528\t\n   529\t    @property\n   530\t    def allow_thread_sharing(self):\n   531\t        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   532\t            return self._thread_sharing_count > 0\n   533\t\n   534\t    def inc_thread_sharing(self):\n   535\t        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   536\t            self._thread_sharing_count += 1\n   537\t\n   538\t    def dec_thread_sharing(self):\n   539\t        with self._thread_sharing_lock:\n   540\t            if self._thread_sharing_count <= 0:\n   541\t                raise RuntimeError('Cannot decrement the thread sharing count below zero.')\n   542\t            self._thread_sharing_count -= 1\n   543\t\n   544\t    def validate_thread_sharing(self):\n   545\t        \"\"\"\n   546\t        Validate that the connection isn't accessed by another thread than the\n   547\t        one which originally created it, unless the connection was explicitly\n   548\t        authorized to be shared between threads (via the `inc_thread_sharing()`\n   549\t        method). Raise an exception if the validation fails.\n   550\t        \"\"\"\n   551\t        if not (self.allow_thread_sharing or self._thread_ident == _thread.get_ident()):\n   552\t            raise DatabaseError(\n   553\t                \"DatabaseWrapper objects created in a \"\n   554\t                \"thread can only be used in that same thread. The object \"\n   555\t                \"with alias '%s' was created in thread id %s and this is \"\n   556\t                \"thread id %s.\"\n   557\t                % (self.alias, self._thread_ident, _thread.get_ident())\n   558\t            )\n   559\t\n   560\t    # ##### Miscellaneous #####\n   561\t\n   562\t    def prepare_database(self):\n   563\t        \"\"\"\n   564\t        Hook to do any database check or preparation, generally called before\n   565\t        migrating a project or an app.\n   566\t        \"\"\"\n   567\t        pass\n   568\t\n   569\t    @cached_property\n   570\t    def wrap_database_errors(self):\n   571\t        \"\"\"\n   572\t        Context manager and decorator that re-throws backend-specific database\n   573\t utils.CursorWrapper(cursor, self)\n   591\t\n   592\t    @contextmanager\n   593\t    def temporary_connection(self):\n   594\t        \"\"\"\n   595\t        Context manager that ensures that a connection is established, and\n   596\t        if it opened one, closes it to avoid leaving a dangling connection.\n   597\t        This is useful for operations outside of the request-response cycle.\n   598\t\n   599\t        Provide a cursor: with self.temporary_connection() as cursor: ...\n   600\t        \"\"\"\n   601\t        must_close = self.connection is None\n   602\t 608\t\n   609\t    @contextmanager\n   610\t    def _nodb_cursor(self):\n   611\t        \"\"\"\n   612\t        Return a cursor from an alternative connection to be used when there is\n   613\t ########################\n   220\t\n   221\t    def __deepcopy__(self, memo):\n   222\t        \"\"\"Don't populate the QuerySet's cache.\"\"\"\n   223\t        obj = self.__class__()\n   224\t        return {**self.__dict__, DJANGO_VERSION_PICKLE_KEY: django.__version__}\n   235\t\n   236\t    def __setstate__(self, state):\n   237\t        pickled_version = data[-1] = \"...(remaining elements truncated)...\"\n   259\t        return '<%s %r>' % (self.__class__.__name__, data)\n   260\t\n   261\t    def __len__(self):\n   262\t        self._fetch_all()\n   263\t        return len(self._result_cache)\n   264\t\n   265\t    def __iter__(self):\n   266\t        \"\"\"\n   267\t        The queryset iterator protocol uses three nested iterators in the\n   268\t        default case:\n   269\t            1. sql.compiler.execute_sql()\n   270\t               - Returns 100 rows at time (constants.GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE)\n   271\t                 using cursor.fetchmany(). This part is responsible for\n   272\t                 doing some column masking, and returning the rows in chunks.\n   273\t            2. sql.compiler.results_iter()\n   274\t               - Returns one row at time. At this point the rows are still just\n   275\t                 tuples. In some cases the return values are converted to\n   276\t                 Python values at this location.\n   277\t            3. self.iterator()\n   278\t               - Responsible for turning the 289\t        if not isinstance(k, (int, slice)):\n   290\t            raise TypeError(\n   291\t                'QuerySet indices must be integers or slices, not %s.'\n   292\t                % is not None and k.start < 0) or\n   298\t                (k.stop is not None and k.stop < 0)\n   299\t            ))\n   300\t        ):\n   301\t            raise ValueError('Negative indexing is not supported.')\n   302\t\n   303\t        if self._result_cache is not None:\n   304\t            return self._result_cache[k]\n   305\t\n   306\t        if isinstance(k, slice):\n   307\t            qs   return list(qs)[::k.step] if k.step else qs\n   318\t\n   319\t        qs = self._chain()\n   320\t        qs.query.set_limits(k, k + 1)\n   321\t        qs._fetch_all()\n   322\t        return self._merge_sanity_check(other)\n   329\t        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):\n   330\t            return other\n   331\t        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):\n   332\t            return self\n   333\t        combined = self._chain()\n   334\t        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)\n   335\t        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.AND)\n   336\t        return combined\n   337\t\n   338\t    def __or__(self, other):\n   339\t        self._merge_sanity_check(other)\n   340\t        if isinstance(self, EmptyQuerySet):\n   341\t            return other\n   342\t        if isinstance(other, EmptyQuerySet):\n   343\t            return self\n   344\t        query = self if self.query.can_filter() else self.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=self.values('pk'))\n   345\t        combined = query._chain()\n   346\t        combined._merge_known_related_objects(other)\n   347\t        if not other.query.can_filter():\n   348\t            other = other.model._base_manager.filter(pk__in=other.values('pk'))\n   349\t        combined.query.combine(other.query, sql.OR)\n   350\t        return _iterator(self, use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size):\n   357\t        yield from self._iterable_class(self, chunked_fetch=use_chunked_fetch, chunk_size=chunk_size)\n   358\t\n   359\t    def iterator(self, **kwargs):\n   370\t        \"\"\"\n   371\t        Return a dictionary containing the calculations (aggregation)\n   372\t        over the current queryset.\n   373\t\n   374\t        If args is present the expression is passed as a kwarg using\n   375\t        the Aggregate object's default alias.\n   376\t        \"\"\"\n   377\t        if self.query.distinct_fields:\n   378\t   for arg in args:\n   381\t            # The default_alias property raises TypeError if default_alias\n   382\t            # can't be set automatically or AttributeError if it isn't an\n   383\t            # attribute.\n   384\t            try:\n   385\t                arg.default_alias\n   386\t            except (AttributeError, TypeError):\n   387\t                raise TypeError(\"Complex aggregates require an alias\")\n   388\t            kwargs[arg.default_alias] = arg\n   389\t\n   390\t        query = self.query.chain()\n   391\t        for (alias, aggregate_expr) in query.get_aggregation(self.db, kwargs)\n   404\t\n   405\t    def count(self):\n   406\t        \"\"\"\n   407\t        Perform a SELECT COUNT() and return the number of records as an\n   408\t        integer.\n   409\t\n   410\t        If the QuerySet is already fully cached, return the length of the\n   411\t        cached results set to avoid multiple SELECT COUNT(*) calls.\n   412\t    def get(self, *args, **kwargs):\n   419\t        \"\"\"\n   420\t        Perform the query and return a single object matching the given\n   421\t        keyword arguments.\n   422\t        \"\"\"\n   423\t        if self.query.combinator and (args or kwargs):\n   424\t            raise NotSupportedError(\n   425\t                'Calling QuerySet.get(...) with filters after %s() is not '\n   426\t                'supported.' % self.query.combinator\n   427\t            )\n   428\t        clone = self._chain() if self.query.combinator else self.filter(*args, **kwargs)\n   429\t connections[clone.db].features.supports_select_for_update_with_limit:\n   433\t            limit = MAX_GET_RESULTS\n   434\t            clone.query.set_limits(high=limit)\n   435\t        num = len(clone)\n   436\t        if num == 1:\n   437\t            return clone._result_cache[0]\n   438\t        if not num:\n   439\t            raise self.model.DoesNotExist(\n   440\t                \"%s matching query does not exist.\" %\n   441\t                self.model._meta.object_name\n   442\t            )\n   443\t        raise self.model.MultipleObjectsReturned(\n   444\t 1),\n   447\t            )\n   448\t        )\n   449\t\n   450\t    def create(self, **kwargs):\n   451\t        \"\"\"\n   452\t        Create a new object with the given kwargs, saving it to the database\n   453\t        and returning the created object.\n   454\t        \"\"\"\n   455\t        obj = self.model(**kwargs)\n   456\t        self._for_write = True\n   457\t        obj.save(force_insert=True, using=self.db)\n   458\t        return obj\n   459\t\n   460\t    def _prepare_for_bulk_create(self, objs):\n   461\t        for obj in objs:\n   462\t            if obj.pk is None:\n   463\t                # Populate new PK values.\n   464\t                obj.pk = obj._meta.pk.get_pk_value_on_save(obj)\n   465\t            obj._prepare_related_fields_for_save(operation_name='bulk_create')\n   466\t\n   467\t    def bulk_create(self, objs, batch_size=None, ignore_conflicts=False):\n   468\t        \"\"\"\n   469\t        Insert each of the instances into the database. Do *not* call\n   470\t        save() on each of the instances, do not send any pre/post_save\n   471\t        signals, and do not set the primary key attribute if it is an\n   472\t        autoincrement field (except if features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True).\n   473\t        Multi-table models are not supported.\n   474\t        \"\"\"\n   475\t        # When you bulk insert you don't get the primary keys back (if it's an\n   476\t        # autoincrement, except if can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert=True), so\n   477\t        # you can't insert into the child tables which references this. There\n   478\t        # are two workarounds:\n   479\t        # 1) This could be implemented if you didn't have an autoincrement pk\n   480\t        # 2) You could do it by doing O(n) normal inserts into the parent\n   481\t        #    tables to get the primary keys back and then doing a single bulk\n   482\t        #    insert into the childmost table.\n   483\t        # We currently set the primary keys on the objects when using\n   484\t        # PostgreSQL via the RETURNING ID clause. It should be possible for\n   485\t        # Oracle as well, but the semantics for extracting the primary keys is\n   486\t        # trickier so it's not done yet.\n   487\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size <= 0:\n   488\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   489\t        # Check that the parents share the same concrete model with the our\n   490\t        # model to detect the inheritance pattern ConcreteGrandParent ->\n   491\t        # MultiTableParent -> ProxyChild. Simply checking self.model._meta.proxy\n   492\t        # would not identify that case as involving multiple tables.\n   493\t        for parent in self.model._meta.get_parent_list():\n   494\t            if parent._meta.concrete_model is not self.model._meta.concrete_model:\n   495\t                raise ValueError(\"Can't bulk create a multi-table inherited model\")\n   496\t        if not objs:\n   497\t            return objs\n   498\t        self._for_write = True\n   499\t        connection = connections[self.db]\n   500\t        opts = self.model._meta\n   501\t        fields = opts.concrete_fields\n   502\t        objs = list(objs)\n   503\t        self._prepare_for_bulk_create(objs)\n   504\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   505\t            objs_with_pk, objs_without_pk = partition(lambda o: o.pk is None, objs)\n   506\t            if objs_with_pk:\n   507\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   508\t                    objs_with_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   509\t                )\n   510\t                for obj_with_pk, results in zip(objs_with_pk, returned_columns):\n   511\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   512\t                        if field != opts.pk:\n   513\t                            setattr(obj_with_pk, field.attname, result)\n   514\t                for obj_with_pk in objs_with_pk:\n   515\t                    obj_with_pk._state.adding = False\n   516\t                    obj_with_pk._state.db = self.db\n   517\t            if objs_without_pk:\n   518\t                fields = [f for f in fields if not isinstance(f, AutoField)]\n   519\t                returned_columns = self._batched_insert(\n   520\t                    objs_without_pk, fields, batch_size, ignore_conflicts=ignore_conflicts,\n   521\t                )\n   522\t                if connection.features.can_return_rows_from_bulk_insert and not ignore_conflicts:\n   523\t                    assert len(returned_columns) == len(objs_without_pk)\n   524\t                for obj_without_pk, results in zip(objs_without_pk, returned_columns):\n   525\t                    for result, field in zip(results, opts.db_returning_fields):\n   526\t                        setattr(obj_without_pk, field.attname, result)\n   527\t                    obj_without_pk._state.adding = False\n   528\t                    obj_without_pk._state.db = self.db\n   529\t\n   530\t        return objs\n   531\t\n   532\t    def bulk_update(self, objs, fields, batch_size=None):\n   533\t        \"\"\"\n   534\t        Update the given fields in each of the given objects in the database.\n   535\t        \"\"\"\n   536\t        if batch_size is not None and batch_size < 0:\n   537\t            raise ValueError('Batch size must be a positive integer.')\n   538\t        if not fields:\n   539\t            raise ValueError('Field names must be given to bulk_update().')\n   540\t        objs = tuple(objs)\n   541\t        if any(obj.pk is None for obj in objs):\n   542\t            raise ValueError('All bulk_update() objects must have a primary key set.')\n   543\t        fields = [self.model._meta.get_field(name) for name in fields]\n   544\t        if any(not f.concrete or f.many_to_many for f in fields):\n   545\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() can only be used with concrete fields.')\n   546\t        if any(f.primary_key for f in fields):\n   547\t            raise ValueError('bulk_update() cannot be used with primary key fields.')\n   548\t        if not objs:\n   549\t            return 0\n   550\t        # PK is used twice in the resulting update query, once in the filter\n   551\t        # and once in the WHEN. Each field will also have one CAST.\n   552\t        max_batch_size = connections[self.db].ops.bulk_batch_size(['pk', 'pk'] + fields, objs)\n   553\t        batch_size = min(batch_size, max_batch_size) if batch_size else max_batch_size\n   554\t        requires_casting = connections[self.db].features.requires_casted_case_in_updates\n   555\t        batches = (objs[i:i + batch_size] for i in range(0, len(objs), batch_size))\n   556\t        updates = []\n   557\t        for batch_objs in batches:\n   558\t            update_kwargs = {}\n   559\t            for field in fields:\n   560\t                when_statements = []\n   561\t                for obj in batch_objs:\n   562\t                    attr = getattr(obj, field.attname)\n   563\t                    if not isinstance(attr, Expression):\n   564\t                        attr = Value(attr, output_field=field)\n   565\t                    when_statements.append(When(pk=obj.pk, then=attr))\n   566\t                case_statement = Case(*when_statements, output_field=field)\n   567\t                if requires_casting:\n   568\t                    case_statement = Cast(case_statement, output_field=field)\n   569\t                update_kwargs[field.attname] = case_statement\n   570\t            updates.append(([obj.pk for obj in batch_objs], update_kwargs))\n   571\t        rows_updated = 0\n   572\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db, savepoint=False):\n   573\t            for pks, update_kwargs in updates:\n   574\t                rows_updated += self.filter(pk__in=pks).update(**update_kwargs)\n   575\t        return rows_updated\n   576\t    bulk_update.alters_data = True\n   577\t\n   578\t    def get_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   579\t        \"\"\"\n   580\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, creating one if necessary.\n   581\t        Return a tuple of (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   582\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   583\t        \"\"\"\n   584\t        # The get() needs to be targeted at the write database in order\n   585\t        # to avoid potential transaction consistency problems.\n   586\t        self._for_write = True\n   587\t        try:\n   588\t            return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   589\t        except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   590\t            params = self._extract_model_params(defaults, **kwargs)\n   591\t            # Try to create an object using passed params.\n   592\t            try:\n   593\t                with transaction.atomic(using=self.db):\n   594\t                    params = dict(resolve_callables(params))\n   595\t                    return self.create(**params), True\n   596\t            except IntegrityError:\n   597\t                try:\n   598\t                    return self.get(**kwargs), False\n   599\t                except self.model.DoesNotExist:\n   600\t                    pass\n   601\t                raise\n   602\t\n   603\t    def update_or_create(self, defaults=None, **kwargs):\n   604\t        \"\"\"\n   605\t        Look up an object with the given kwargs, updating one with defaults\n   606\t        if it exists, otherwise create a new one.\n   607\t        Return a tuple (object, created), where created is a boolean\n   608\t        specifying whether an object was created.\n   609\t        \"\"\"\n   610\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   611\t        self._for_write = True\n   612\t        with transaction.atomic(using=self.db):\n   613\t            # Lock the row so that a concurrent update is blocked until\n   614\t            # update_or_create() has performed its save.\n   615\t            obj, created = self.select_for_update().get_or_create(defaults, **kwargs)\n   616\t            if created:\n   617\t                return obj, created\n   618\t            for k, v in resolve_callables(defaults):\n   619\t                setattr(obj, k, v)\n   620\t            obj.save(using=self.db)\n   621\t        return obj, False\n   622\t\n   623\t    def _extract_model_params(self, defaults, **kwargs):\n   624\t        \"\"\"\n   625\t        Prepare `params` for creating a model instance based on the given\n   626\t        kwargs; for use by get_or_create().\n   627\t        \"\"\"\n   628\t        defaults = defaults or {}\n   629\t        params = {k: v for k, v in kwargs.items() if LOOKUP_SEP not in k}\n   630\t        params.update(defaults)\n   631\t        property_names = self.model._meta._property_names\n   632\t        invalid_params = []\n   633\t        for param in params:\n   634\t            try:\n   635\t                self.model._meta.get_field(param)\n   636\t            except exceptions.FieldDoesNotExist:\n   637\t                # It's okay to use a model's property if it has a setter.\n   638\t                if not (param in property_names and getattr(self.model, param).fset):\n   639\t                    invalid_params.append(param)\n   640\t        if invalid_params:\n   641\t            raise exceptions.FieldError(\n   642\t                \"Invalid field name(s) for model %s: '%s'.\" % (\n   643\t                    self.model._meta.object_name,\n   644\t                    \"', '\".join(sorted(invalid_params)),\n   645\t                ))\n   646\t        return params\n   647\t\n   648\t    def _earliest(self, *fields):\n   649\t        \"\"\"\n   650\t        Return the earliest object according to fields (if given) or by the\n   651\t        model's Meta.get_latest_by.\n   652\t        \"\"\"\n   653\t        if fields:\n   654\t            order_by = fields\n   655\t        else:\n   656\t            order_by = getattr(self.model._meta, 'get_latest_by')\n   657\t            if order_by and not isinstance(order_by, (tuple, list)):\n   658\t                order_by = (order_by,)\n   659\t        if order_by is None:\n   660\t            raise ValueError(\n   661\t                \"earliest() and latest() require either fields as positional \"\n   662\t                \"arguments or 'get_latest_by' in the model's Meta.\"\n   663\t            )\n   664\t        obj = self._chain()\n   665\t        obj.query.set_limits(high=1)\n   666\t        obj.query.clear_ordering(force=True)\n   667\t        obj.query.add_ordering(*order_by)\n   668\t        return obj.get()\n   669\t\n   670\t    def earliest(self, *fields):\n   671\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   672\t            raise TypeError('Cannot change a query once a slice has been taken.')\n   673\t        return self._earliest(*fields)\n   674\t\n   675\t    def latest(self, *fields):\n   676\t        if self.query.is_sliced:\n   677\t            raise TypeError('Cannot change a query once a slice has been taken.')\n   678\t        return self.reverse()._earliest(*fields)\n   679\t\n   680\t    def first(self):\n   681\t        \"\"\"Return the first object of a query or None if no match is found.\"\"\"\n   682\t        for obj in (self if self.ordered else self.order_by('pk'))[:1]:\n   683\t            return obj\n   684\t\n   685\t    def last(self):\n   686\t        \"\"\"Return the last object of a query or None if no match We've\n    12\t:ref:`begun the deprecation process for some features\n    13\t<deprecated-features-4.0>`.\n    14\t\n    15\tSee the :doc:`/howto/upgrade-version` guide if you're updating an existing\n    16\tproject.\n    17\t\n    18\tPython compatibility\n    19\t====================\n    20\t\n    21\tDjango 4.0 supports Python 3.8, 3.9, and 3.10. We **highly recommend** and only\n    22\tofficially support the latest release of each series.\n    23\t\n    24\tThe Django 3.2.x series is the last to support Python 3.6 and 3.7.\n    25\t\n    26\t.. _whats-new-4.0:\n    27\t\n    28\tWhat's new in Django 4.0\n    29\t========================\n    30\t\n    31\tFunctional unique constraints\n    32\t-----------------------------\n    33\t\n    34\tThe new :attr:`*expressions <django.db.models.UniqueConstraint.expressions>`\n    35\tpositional argument of\n    36\t:class:`UniqueConstraint() <django.db.models.UniqueConstraint>` enables\n    37\tcreating functional unique constraints on expressions and database functions.\n    38\tFor example::\n    39\t\n    40\t    from django.db import models\n    41\t    from django.db.models import UniqueConstraint\n    42\t    from django.db.models.functions import Lower\n    43\t\n    44\t\n    45\t    class MyModel(models.Model):\n    46\t        first_name = models.CharField(max_length=255)\n    47\t        last_name = models.CharField(max_length=255)\n    48\t\n    49\t        class Meta:\n    50\t            indexes = [\n    51\t                UniqueConstraint(\n    52\t                    Lower('first_name'),\n    53\t                    Lower('last_name').desc(),\n    54\t                    name='first_last_name_unique',\n    55\t                ),\n    56\t            ]\n    57\t\n    58\tFunctional unique constraints are added to models using the\n    59\t:attr:`Meta.constraints <django.db.models.Options.constraints>` option.\n    60\t\n    61\t``scrypt`` password hasher\n    62\t--------------------------\n    63\t\n    64\tThe new :ref:`scrypt password hasher <scrypt-usage>` is more secure and\n    65\trecommended over PBKDF2. However, it's not the default as it requires OpenSSL\n    66\t1.1+ and more memory.\n    67\t\n    68\tRedis cache backend\n    69\t-------------------\n    70\t\n    71\tThe new ``django.core.cache.backends.redis.RedisCache`` cache backend provides\n    72\tbuilt-in support for caching with Redis. `redis-py`_ 3.0.0 or higher is\n    73\trequired. For more details, see the :ref:`documentation on caching with Redis\n    74\tin Django <redis>`.\n    75\t\n    76\t.. _`redis-py`: https://pypi.org/project/redis/\n    77\t\n    78\tMinor features\n    79\t--------------\n    80\t\n    81\t:mod:`django.contrib.admin`\n    82\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n    83\t\n    84\t* The ``admin/base.html`` template now has a new block ``header`` which\n    85\t  contains the admin site header.\n    86\t\n    87\t* The new :meth:`.ModelAdmin.get_formset_kwargs` method allows customizing the\n    88\t  keyword arguments passed to the constructor of a formset.\n    89\t\n    90\t* The navigation sidebar now has a quick filter toolbar.\n    91\t\n    92\t* The new context variable ``model`` which contains the model class for each\n    93\t  model is  97\t\n    98\t* The :attr:`.InlineModelAdmin.verbose_name_plural` attribute now fallbacks to\n    99\t  the :attr:`.InlineModelAdmin.verbose_name` + ``'s'``.\n   100\t\n begins\n   204\t  the process of making cache backends async-compatible. The new async methods\n   205\t  all have ``a`` prefixed names, e.g. ``aadd()``, ``aget()``, ``aset()``,\n   206\t  ``aget_or_set()``, or ``adelete_many()``.\n   207\t\n   208\t  Going forward, the ``a`` prefix will be used for async variants of methods\n   209\t  generally.\n   210\t\n   211\tCSRF\n   212\t~~~~\n   213\t\n   214\t* CSRF protection now consults the ``Origin`` header, if present. This allows custom error messages to use the ``%(value)s``\n   250\t  placeholder.\n   251\t\n   252\t* :class:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet` now renders non-form errors with\n   253\t  an additional class of ``nonform`` to help distinguish them from\n   254\t  form-specific errors.\n   255\t\n   256\t* :class:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet` now allows customizing the widget\n   257\t  used when deleting forms via\n   258\t  :attr:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.can_delete` by setting the\n   259\t  :attr:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.deletion_widget` attribute or\n   260\t  overriding :meth:`~django.forms.formsets.BaseFormSet.get_deletion_widget`\n   261\t  method.\n   262\t\n   263\tGeneric Views\n   264\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   265\t\n   266\t* :class:`~django.views.generic.edit.DeleteView` now uses\n   267\t  :class:`~django.views.generic.edit.FormMixin`, allowing you to provide a\n   268\t  :class:`~django.forms.Form` subclass, with a checkbox for example, to confirm\n   269\t  deletion. This allows loading shell history between interactive sessions.\n   293\t  As a consequence, ``readline`` is no longer loaded if running in *isolated*\n   294\t  mode.\n   295\t\n   296\t* The new :attr:`BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments\n   297\t  <django.core.management.BaseCommand.suppressed_base_arguments>` attribute\n   298\t  allows suppressing unsupported default command options in the help output.\n   299\t\n   300\t* The new :option:`startapp --exclude` and :option:`startproject --exclude`\n   301\t  options allow excluding directories from the template.\n   302\t\n   303\tMigrations\n   304\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   305\t\n   306\t* ...\n   307\t\n   308\tModels\n   309\t~~~~~~\n   310\t\n   311\t* New :meth:`QuerySet.contains(obj) <.QuerySet.contains>` method returns\n   312\t  whether the queryset contains the given object. This tries to perform the\n   313\t  query in the simplest and fastest way possible.\n   314\t\n   315\t* The new ``precision`` argument of the\n   316\t  :class:`Round() <django.db.models.functions.Round>` database function allows\n   317\t  specifying the number of decimal places after rounding.\n   318\t\n   319\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_create` now sets the primary key on objects when using\n   320\t  SQLite 3.35+.\n   321\t\n   322\t* :class:`~django.db.models.DurationField` now supports multiplying and\n   323\t  dividing by scalar values on SQLite.\n   324\t\n   325\t* :meth:`.QuerySet.bulk_update` now returns the number of objects updated.\n   326\t\n   327\t* The new :attr:`.Aggregate.empty_aggregate_value` attribute allows specifying\n   328\t  a value to return when the aggregation is used over an empty result set.\n   329\t\n   330\t* The ``skip_locked`` argument of :meth:`.QuerySet.select_for_update()` is now\n   331\t  allowed on MariaDB 10.6+.\n   332\t\n   333\t* :class:`~django.db.models.Lookup` expressions may now be used in ``QuerySet``\n   334\t  annotations, aggregations, and directly in filters.\n   335\t\n   336\t* The new :ref:`default <aggregate-default>` argument for built-in aggregates\n   337\t  allows specifying a value to be returned when the queryset (or grouping)\n   338\t  contains no entries, rather than ``None``.\n   339\t\n   340\tRequests and Responses\n   341\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   342\t\n   343\t* The :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` now adds the\n   344\t  :ref:`Cross-Origin Opener Policy <cross-origin-opener-policy>` header with a\n   345\t  value of ``'same-origin'`` to prevent cross-origin popups from sharing the\n   346\t  same browsing context. You can prevent this header from being added by\n   347\t  setting the :setting:`SECURE_CROSS_ORIGIN_OPENER_POLICY` setting to ``None``.\n   348\t\n   349\tSecurity\n   350\t~~~~~~~~\n   351\t\n   352\t* ...\n   353\t\n   354\tSerialization\n   355\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   356\t\n   357\t* ...\n   358\t\n   359\tSignals\n   360\t~~~~~~~\n   361\t\n   362\t* The new ``stdout`` argument for :func:`~django.db.models.signals.pre_migrate`\n   363\t  and :func:`~django.db.models.signals.post_migrate` signals allows redirecting\n   364\t  output to a stream-like object. captures new callbacks added\n   399\t  while executing :func:`.transaction.on_commit` callbacks.\n   400\t\n   401\tURLs\n   402\t~~~~\n   403\t\n   404\t* ...\n   405\t\n   406\tUtilities\n   407\t~~~~~~~~~\n   408\t\n   409\t* ...\n   410\t\n   411\tValidators\n   412\t~~~~~~~~~~\n   413\t\n   414\t* ...\n   415\t\n   416\t.. _backwards-incompatible-4.0:\n   417\t\n   418\tBackwards incompatible changes in 4.0\n   419\t=====================================\n   420\t\n   421\tDatabase backend API\n   422\t--------------------\n   423\t\n   424\tThis section describes changes that may be needed in third-party database\n   425\tbackends.\n   426\t\n   427\t* ``DatabaseOperations.year_lookup_bounds_for_date_field()`` and\n   428\t  ``year_lookup_bounds_for_datetime_field()`` methods now take the optional\n   429\t  ``iso_year`` argument in order to support bounds for ISO-8601 week-numbering\n   430\t  years.\n   431\t\n   432\t* The second argument of ``DatabaseSchemaEditor._unique_sql()`` and\n   433\t  ``_create_unique_sql()`` methods is now fields instead of columns.\n   434\t\n   435\t:mod:`django.contrib.gis`\n   436\t-------------------------\n   437\t\n   438\t* Support for PostGIS 2.3 is removed.\n   439\t\n   440\t* Support for GDAL 2.0 and GEOS 3.5 is removed.\n   441\t\n   442\tDropped support for PostgreSQL 9.6\n   443\t----------------------------------\n   444\t\n   445\tUpstream support for PostgreSQL 9.6 ends in November 2021. Django 4.0 supports\n   446\tPostgreSQL 10 and higher.\n   447\t\n   448\tDropped support for Oracle 12.2 and 18c\n   449\t---------------------------------------\n   450\t\n   451\tUpstream support for Oracle 12.2 ends in March 2022 and for Oracle 18c it ends\n   452\tin June 2021. Django 3.2 will be supported until April 2024. Django 4.0\n   453\tofficially supports Oracle 19c.\n   454\t\n   455\t.. _csrf-trusted-origins-changes-4.0:\n   456\t\n   457\t``CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`` changes\n   458\t--------------------------------\n   459\t\n   460\tFormat change\n   461\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   462\t\n   463\tValues in the :setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` setting must include the scheme\n   464\t(e.g. ``'http://'`` or ``'https://'``) instead of only the hostname.\n   465\t\n   466\tAlso, values that started with a dot, must now also include an asterisk before\n   467\tthe dot. For example, change ``'.example.com'`` to ``'https://*.example.com'``.\n   468\t\n   469\tA system check detects any required changes.\n   470\t\n   471\tConfiguring it may now be required\n   472\t~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n   473\t\n   474\tAs CSRF protection now consults the ``Origin`` header, you may need to set\n   475\t:setting:`CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS`, particularly if you allow requests from\n   476\tsubdomains by setting :setting:`CSRF_COOKIE_DOMAIN` (or\n   477\t:setting:`SESSION_COOKIE_DOMAIN` if :setting:`CSRF_USE_SESSIONS` is enabled) to\n   478\ta value starting with a dot.\n   479\t\n   480\t``SecurityMiddleware`` no longer sets the ``X-XSS-Protection`` header\n   481\t---------------------------------------------------------------------\n   482\t\n   483\tThe :class:`~django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware` no longer sets the\n   484\t``X-XSS-Protection`` header if the ``SECURE_BROWSER_XSS_FILTER`` setting is\n   485\t``True``. The setting is removed.\n   486\t\n   487\tMost modern browsers don't honor the ``X-XSS-Protection`` HTTP header. You can\n   488\tuse Content-Security-Policy_ without allowing ``'unsafe-inline'`` scripts\n   489\tinstead.\n   490\t\n   491\tIf you want to support legacy browsers and set the header, use this line in a\n   492\tcustom middleware::\n   493\t\n   494\t    response.headers.setdefault('X-XSS-Protection', '1; mode=block')\n   495\t\n   496\t.. _Content-Security-Policy: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Content-Security-Policy\n   497\t\n   498\tMigrations autodetector changes\n   499\t-------------------------------\n   500\t\n   501\tThe migrations autodetector now uses model states instead of model classes.\n   502\tAlso, migration operations for ``ForeignKey`` and ``ManyToManyField`` fields no\n   503\tlonger specify attributes which were not passed to the fields during\n   504\tinitialization.\n   505\t\n   506\tAs a side-effect, running ``makemigrations`` might generate no-op\n   507\t``AlterField`` operations for ``ManyToManyField`` and ``ForeignKey`` fields in\n   508\tsome cases.\n   509\t\n   510\tMiscellaneous\n   511\t-------------\n   512\t\n   513\t* Support for ``cx_Oracle`` < 7.0 is removed.\n   514\t\n   515\t* To allow serving a Django site on a subpath without changing the value of\n   516\t  :setting:`STATIC_URL`, the leading slash is removed from that setting (now\n   517\t  ``'static/'``) in the default :djadmin:`startproject` template.\n   518\t\n   519\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.admin.AdminSite` method for the admin ``index``\n   520\t  view is no longer decorated with ``never_cache`` when accessed directly,\n   521\t  rather than via the recommended ``AdminSite.urls`` property, or\n   522\t  ``AdminSite.get_urls()`` method.\n   523\t\n   524\t* Unsupported operations on a sliced queryset now raise ``TypeError`` instead\n   525\t  of ``AssertionError``.\n   526\t\n   527\t* The undocumented ``django.test.runner.reorder_suite()`` function is renamed\n   528\t  to ``reorder_tests()``. It now accepts an iterable of tests rather than a\n   529\t  test suite, and returns an iterator of tests.\n   530\t\n   531\t* Calling ``FileSystemStorage.delete()`` with an empty ``name`` now raises\n   532\t  ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   533\t\n   534\t* Calling ``EmailMultiAlternatives.attach_alternative()`` or\n   535\t  ``EmailMessage.attach()`` with an invalid ``content`` or ``mimetype``\n   536\t  arguments now raise ``ValueError`` instead of ``AssertionError``.\n   537\t\n   538\t* :meth:`~django.test.SimpleTestCase.assertHTMLEqual` no longer considers a\n   539\t  non-boolean attribute without a value equal to an attribute with the same\n   540\t  name and value.\n   541\t\n   542\t* Tests that fail to load, for example due to syntax errors, now always match\n   543\t  when using :option:`test --tag`.\n   544\t\n   545\t* The undocumented ``django.contrib.admin.utils.lookup_needs_distinct()``\n   546\t  function is renamed to ``lookup_spawns_duplicates()``.\n   547\t\n   548\t* The undocumented ``HttpRequest.get_raw_uri()`` method is removed. The\n   549\t  :meth:`.HttpRequest.build_absolute_uri` method may be a suitable alternative.\n   550\t\n   551\t* The ``object`` argument of undocumented ``ModelAdmin.log_addition()``,\n   552\t  ``log_change()``, and ``log_deletion()`` methods is renamed to ``obj``.\n   553\t\n   554\t* :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.RssFeed`,\n   555\t  :class:`~django.utils.feedgenerator.Atom1Feed`, and their subclasses now\n   556\t  emit elements with no content as self-closing tags.\n   557\t\n   558\t* ``NodeList.render()`` no longer casts the output of ``render()`` method for\n   559\t  individual nodes to a string. ``Node.render()`` should always return a string\n   560\t  as documented.\n   561\t\n   562\t* The ``where_class`` property of ``django.db.models.sql.query.Query`` and the\n   563\t  ``where_class`` argument to the private ``get_extra_restriction()`` method of\n   564\t  ``ForeignObject`` and ``ForeignObjectRel`` are removed. If needed, initialize\n   565\t  ``django.db.models.sql.where.WhereNode`` instead.\n   566\t\n   567\t* The ``filter_clause`` argument of the undocumented ``Query.add_filter()``\n   568\t  method is replaced by two positional arguments ``filter_lhs`` and\n   569\t  ``filter_rhs``.\n   570\t\n   571\t* :class:`~django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware` now uses\n   572\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_NEEDS_UPDATE']`` in place of\n   573\t  ``request.META['CSRF_COOKIE_USED']``, ``request.csrf_cookie_needs_reset``,\n   574\t  and ``response.csrf_cookie_set`` to track whether the CSRF cookie should be\n   575\t  sent. This is an undocumented, private API.\n   576\t\n   577\t* The undocumented ``TRANSLATOR_COMMENT_MARK`` constant is moved from\n   578\t  ``django.template.base`` to ``django.utils.translation.template``.\n   579\t\n   580\t* The ``real_apps`` argument of the undocumented\n   581\t  ``django.db.migrations.state.ProjectState.__init__()`` method must now be a\n   582\t  set if provided.\n   583\t\n   584\t* :class:`~django.forms.RadioSelect` and\n   585\t  :class:`~django.forms.CheckboxSelectMultiple` widgets are now rendered in\n   586\t  ``<div>`` tags so they are announced more concisely by screen readers. If you\n   587\t  need the previous behavior, :ref:`override the widget template\n   588\t  <overriding-built-in-widget-templates>` with the appropriate template from\n   589\t  Django 3.2.\n   590\t\n   591\t* The :tfilter:`floatformat` template filter no longer depends on the\n   592\t  ``USE_L10N`` setting and always returns localized output. Use the ``u``\n   593\t  suffix to disable localization.\n   594\t\n   595\t* The default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting is changed to ``True``. See the\n   596\t  :ref:`Localization section <use_l10n_deprecation>` above for more details.\n   597\t\n   598\t.. _deprecated-features-4.0:\n   599\t\n   600\tFeatures deprecated in 4.0\n   601\t==========================\n   602\t\n   603\tTime zone support\n   604\t-----------------\n   605\t\n   606\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the :setting:`USE_TZ`\n   607\tsetting will change from ``False`` to ``True``, and time zone support will be\n   608\tenabled by default, in Django 5.0.\n   609\t\n   610\tNote that the default :file:`settings.py` file created by\n   611\t:djadmin:`django-admin startproject <startproject>` includes\n   612\t:setting:`USE_TZ = True <USE_TZ>` since Django 1.4.\n   613\t\n   614\tYou can set ``USE_TZ`` to ``False`` in your project settings before then to\n   615\topt-out.\n   616\t\n   617\t.. _use_l10n_deprecation:\n   618\t\n   619\tLocalization\n   620\t------------\n   621\t\n   622\tIn order to follow good practice, the default value of the ``USE_L10N`` setting\n   623\tis changed from ``False`` to ``True``.\n   624\t\n   625\tMoreover ``USE_L10N`` is deprecated as of this release. Starting with Django\n   626\t5.0, by default, any date or number displayed by Django will be localized.\n   627\t\n   628\tThe :ttag:`{% localize %} <localize>` tag and the :tfilter:`localize`/\n   629\t:tfilter:`unlocalize` filters will still be honored by Django.\n   630\t\n   631\tMiscellaneous\n   632\t-------------\n   633\t\n   634\t* ``SERIALIZE`` test setting is deprecated as it can be inferred from the\n   635\t  :attr:`~django.test.TestCase.databases` with the\n   636\t  :ref:`serialized_rollback <test-case-serialized-rollback>` option enabled.\n   637\t\n   638\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.baseconv`` module is deprecated.\n   639\t\n   640\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.datetime_safe`` module is deprecated.\n   641\t\n   642\t* The default sitemap protocol for sitemaps built outside the context of a\n   643\t  request will change from ``'http'`` to ``'https'`` in Django 5.0.\n   644\t\n   645\t* The ``extra_tests`` argument for :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.build_suite` and\n   646\t  :meth:`.DiscoverRunner.run_tests` is deprecated.\n   647\t\n   648\t* The :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.ArrayAgg`,\n   649\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.JSONBAgg`, and\n   650\t  :class:`~django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.StringAgg` aggregates will return\n   651\t  ``None`` when there are no rows instead of ``[]``, ``[]``, and ``''``\n   652\t  respectively in Django 5.0. If you need the previous behavior, explicitly set\n   653\t  ``default`` to ``Value([])``, ``Value('[]')``, or ``Value('')``.\n   654\t\n   655\tFeatures removed in 4.0\n   656\t=======================\n   657\t\n   658\tThese features have reached the end of their deprecation cycle and are removed\n   659\tin Django 4.0.\n   660\t\n   661\tSee :ref:`deprecated-features-3.0` for details on these changes, including how\n   662\tto remove usage of these features.\n   663\t\n   664\t* ``django.utils.http.urlquote()``, ``urlquote_plus()``, ``urlunquote()``, and\n   665\t  ``urlunquote_plus()`` are removed.\n   666\t\n   667\t* ``django.utils.encoding.force_text()`` and ``smart_text()`` are removed.\n   668\t\n   669\t* ``django.utils.translation.ugettext()``, ``ugettext_lazy()``,\n   670\t  ``ugettext_noop()``, ``ungettext()``, and ``ungettext_lazy()`` are removed.\n   671\t\n   672\t* ``django.views.i18n.set_language()`` doesn't set the user language in\n   673\t  ``request.session`` (key ``_language``).\n   674\t\n   675\t* ``alias=None`` is required in the signature of\n   676\t  ``django.db.models.Expression.get_group_by_cols()`` subclasses.\n   677\t\n   678\t* ``django.utils.text.unescape_entities()`` is removed.\n   679\t\n   680\t* ``django.utils.http.is_safe_url()`` is removed.\n   681\t\n   682\tSee :ref:`deprecated-features-3.1` for details on these changes, including how\n   683\tto remove usage of these features.\n   684\t\n   685\t* The ``PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT_DAYS`` setting is removed.\n   686\t\n   687\t* The :lookup:`isnull` lookup no longer allows using non-boolean values as the\n   688\t  right-hand side.\n   689\t\n   690\t* The ``django.db.models.query_utils.InvalidQuery`` exception class is removed.\n   691\t\n   692\t* The ``django-admin.py`` entry point is removed.\n   693\t\n   694\t* The ``HttpRequest.is_ajax()`` method is removed.\n   695\t\n   696\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of cookies values used by\n   697\t  ``django.contrib.messages.storage.cookie.CookieStorage`` is removed.\n   698\t\n   699\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 password reset tokens in the admin site (that\n   700\t  use the SHA-1 hashing algorithm) is removed.\n   701\t\n   702\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of sessions is removed.\n   703\t\n   704\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.Signer`` signatures\n   705\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) is removed.\n   706\t\n   707\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.dumps()`` signatures\n   708\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) in ``django.core.signing.loads()`` is\n   709\t  removed.\n   710\t\n   711\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 user sessions (that use the SHA-1 algorithm)\n   712\t  is removed.\n   713\t\n   714\t* The ``get_response`` argument for\n   715\t  ``django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin.__init__()`` is required and\n   716\t  doesn't accept ``None``.\n   717\t\n   718\t* The ``providing_args`` argument for ``django.dispatch.Signal`` is removed.\n   719\t\n   720\t* The ``length`` argument for ``django.utils.crypto.get_random_string()`` is\n   721\t  required.\n   722\t\n   723\t* The ``list`` message for ``ModelMultipleChoiceField`` is removed.\n   724\t\n   725\t* Support for passing raw column aliases to ``QuerySet.order_by()`` is removed.\n   726\t\n   727\t* The ``NullBooleanField`` model field is removed, except for support in\n   728\t  historical migrations.\n   729\t\n   730\t* ``django.conf.urls.url()`` is removed.\n   731\t\n   732\t* The ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.JSONField`` model field is removed,\n   733\t  except for support in historical migrations.\n   734\t\n   735\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.jsonb.KeyTransform`` and\n   736\t  ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.jsonb.KeyTextTransform`` are removed.\n   737\t\n   738\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.forms.JSONField`` is removed.\n   739\t\n   740\t* The ``{% ifequal %}`` and ``{% ifnotequal %}`` template tags are removed.\n   741\t\n More details\n     8\tabout each item can often be found in the release notes of two versions prior.\n     9\t\n    10\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-5.0:\n    11\t\n    12\t5.0\n    13\t---\n    14\t\n    15\tSee the :ref:`Django 4.0 release notes <deprecated-features-4.0>` for more\n    16\tdetails on these changes.\n    17\t\n    18\t* The ``SERIALIZE`` test setting will be removed.\n    19\t\n    20\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.baseconv`` module will be removed.\n    21\t\n    22\t* The undocumented ``django.utils.datetime_safe`` module will be removed.\n    23\t\n    24\t* The default value of the ``USE_TZ`` setting will change from ``False`` to\n    25\t  ``True``.\n    26\t\n    27\t* The default sitemap protocol for sitemaps built outside the context of a\n    28\t  request will change from ``'http'`` to ``'https'``.\n    29\t\n    30\t* The ``extra_tests`` argument for ``DiscoverRunner.build_suite()`` and\n    31\t  ``DiscoverRunner.run_tests()`` will be removed.\n    32\t\n    33\t* The ``django.contrib.postgres.aggregates.ArrayAgg``, ``JSONBAgg``, and\n    34\t  ``StringAgg`` aggregates will return ``None`` when there are no rows instead\n    35\t  of ``[]``, ``[]``, and ``''`` respectively.\n    36\t\n    37\t* The ``USE_L10N`` setting will be removed.\n    38\t\n    39\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-4.1:\n    40\t\n    41\t4.1\n    42\t---\n    43\t\n    44\tSee the :ref:`Django 3.2 release notes <deprecated-features-3.2>` for more\n    45\tdetails on these changes.\n    46\t\n    47\t* Support for assigning objects which don't support creating deep copies with\n    48\t  ``copy.deepcopy()`` to class attributes in ``TestCase.setUpTestData()`` will\n    49\t  be removed.\n    50\t\n    51\t* ``BaseCommand.requires_system_checks`` won't support boolean values.\n    52\t\n    53\t* The ``whitelist`` argument and ``domain_whitelist`` attribute of\n    54\t  ``django.core.validators.EmailValidator`` will be removed.\n    55\t\n    56\t* The ``default_app_config`` module variable will be removed.\n    57\t\n    58\t* ``TransactionTestCase.assertQuerysetEqual()`` will no longer automatically\n    59\t  call ``repr()`` on a queryset when compared to string values.\n    60\t\n    61\t* ``django.core.cache.backends.memcached.MemcachedCache`` will be removed.\n    62\t\n    63\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.2 format of messages used by\n    64\t  ``django.contrib.messages.storage.cookie.CookieStorage`` will be removed.\n    65\t\n    66\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-4.0:\n    67\t\n    68\t4.0\n    69\t---\n    70\t\n    71\tSee the :ref:`Django 3.0 release notes <deprecated-features-3.0>` for more\n    72\tdetails on these changes.\n    73\t\n    74\t* ``django.utils.http.urlquote()``, ``urlquote_plus()``, ``urlunquote()``, and\n    75\t  ``urlunquote_plus()`` will be removed.\n    76\t\n    77\t* ``django.utils.encoding.force_text()`` and ``smart_text()`` will be removed.\n    78\t\n    79\t* ``django.utils.translation.ugettext()``, ``ugettext_lazy()``,\n    80\t  ``ugettext_noop()``, ``ungettext()``, and ``ungettext_lazy()`` will be\n    81\t  removed.\n    82\t\n    83\t* ``django.views.i18n.set_language()`` will no longer set the user language in\n    84\t  ``request.session`` (key ``django.utils.translation.LANGUAGE_SESSION_KEY``).\n    85\t\n    86\t* ``alias=None`` will be required in the signature of\n    87\t  ``django.db.models.Expression.get_group_by_cols()`` subclasses.\n    88\t\n    89\t* ``django.utils.text.unescape_entities()`` will be removed.\n    90\t\n    91\t* ``django.utils.http.is_safe_url()`` will be removed.\n    92\t\n    93\tSee the :ref:`Django 3.1 release notes <deprecated-features-3.1>` for more\n    94\tdetails on these changes.\n    95\t\n    96\t* The ``PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT_DAYS`` setting will be removed.\n    97\t\n    98\t* The undocumented usage of the :lookup:`isnull` lookup with non-boolean values\n    99\t  as the right-hand side will no longer be allowed.\n   100\t\n   101\t* The ``django.db.models.query_utils.InvalidQuery`` exception class will be\n   102\t  removed.\n   103\t\n   104\t* The ``django-admin.py`` entry point will be removed.\n   105\t\n   106\t* The ``HttpRequest.is_ajax()`` method will be removed.\n   107\t\n   108\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of cookies values used by\n   109\t  ``django.contrib.messages.storage.cookie.CookieStorage`` will be removed.\n   110\t\n   111\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 password reset tokens in the admin site (that\n   112\t  use the SHA-1 hashing algorithm) will be removed.\n   113\t\n   114\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 encoding format of sessions will be removed.\n   115\t\n   116\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.Signer`` signatures\n   117\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) will be removed.\n   118\t\n   119\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 ``django.core.signing.dumps()`` signatures\n   120\t  (encoded with the SHA-1 algorithm) in ``django.core.signing.loads()`` will be\n   121\t  removed.\n   122\t\n   123\t* Support for the pre-Django 3.1 user sessions (that use the SHA-1 algorithm)\n   124\t  will be removed.\n   125\t\n   126\t* The ``get_response`` argument for\n   127\t  ``django.utils.deprecation.MiddlewareMixin.__init__()`` will be required and\n   128\t  won't accept ``None``.\n   129\t\n   130\t* The ``providing_args`` argument for ``django.dispatch.Signal`` will be\n   131\t  removed.\n   132\t\n   133\t* The ``length`` argument for ``django.utils.crypto.get_random_string()`` will\n   134\t  be required.\n   135\t\n   136\t* The ``list`` message for ``ModelMultipleChoiceField`` will be removed.\n   137\t\n   138\t* Support for passing raw column aliases to ``QuerySet.order_by()`` will be\n   139\t  removed.\n   140\t\n   141\t* The model ``NullBooleanField`` will be removed. A stub field will remain for\n   142\t  compatibility with historical migrations.\n   143\t\n   144\t* ``django.conf.urls.url()`` will be removed.\n   145\t\n   146\t* The model ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.JSONField`` will be removed. A\n   147\t  stub field will remain for compatibility with historical migrations.\n   148\t\n   149\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.forms.JSONField``,\n   150\t ifnotequal %}`` template tags will be removed.\n   154\t\n   155\t* The ``DEFAULT_HASHING_ALGORITHM`` transitional setting will be removed.\n   156\t\n   157\t.. _deprecation-removed-in-3.1:\n affect ``GROUP BY`` queries.\n   170\t\n   171\t* ``django.contrib.postgres.fields.FloatRangeField`` and\n   172\t  ``django.contrib.postgres.forms.FloatRangeField`` will be removed.\n   173\t\n
