'5.3':\\n                self.assertEqual(obj.dt.replace(tzinfo=UTC), dt)\\n            else:\\n                self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\\n\\n\\n# RemovedInDjango50Warning: When the deprecation ends, remove setUpClass() and\\n# USE_L10N=False. The tests should remain because format-related settings will\\n# take precedence over locale-dictated formats.\\n@override_settings(DATETIME_FORMAT='c', TIME_ZONE='Africa/Nairobi', USE_L10N=False, USE_TZ=True)\\nclass TemplateTests(SimpleTestCase):\\n    @classmethod\\n    def setUpClass(cls):\\n        with ignore_warnings(category=RemovedInDjango50Warning):\\n            super().setUpClass()\\n\\n    @requires_tz_support\\n    def test_localtime_templatetag_and_filters(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Test the {% localtime %} templatetag and related filters.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        datetimes = {\\n            'utc': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC),\\n            'eat': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30, tzinfo=EAT),\\n            'ict': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, tzinfo=ICT),\\n            'naive': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30),\\n        }\\n        templates = {\\n            'notag': Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{{ dt }}|{{ dt|localtime }}|{{ dt|utc }}|{{ dt|timezone:ICT }}\\\"),\\n            'noarg': Template(\\n                \\\"{% load tz %}{% localtime %}{{ dt }}|{{ dt|localtime }}|\\\"\\n                \\\"{{ dt|utc }}|{{ dt|timezone:ICT }}{% endlocaltime %}\\\"\\n            ),\\n            'on': Template(\\n                \\\"{% load tz %}{% localtime on %}{{ dt }}|{{ dt|localtime }}|\\\"\\n                \\\"{{ dt|utc }}|{{ dt|timezone:ICT }}{% endlocaltime %}\\\"\\n            ),\\n            'off': Template(\\n                \\\"{% load tz %}{% localtime off %}{{ dt }}|{{ dt|localtime }}|\\\"\\n                \\\"{{ dt|utc }}|{{ dt|timezone:ICT }}{% endlocaltime %}\\\"\\n            ),\\n        }\\n\\n        # Transform a list of keys in 'datetimes' to the expected template\\n        # output. This makes the definition of 'results' more readable.\\n        def t(*result):\\n            return '|'.join(datetimes[key].isoformat() for key in result)\\n\\n        # Results for USE_TZ = True\\n\\n        results = {\\n            'utc': {\\n                'notag': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'noarg': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'on': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'off': t('utc', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n            },\\n            'eat': {\\n                'notag': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'noarg': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'on': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'off': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n            },\\n            'ict': {\\n                'notag': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'noarg': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'on': t('eat', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'off': t('ict', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n            },\\n            'naive': {\\n                'notag': t('naive', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'noarg': t('naive', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'on': t('naive', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n                'off': t('naive', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict'),\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        for k1, dt in datetimes.items():\\n            for k2, tpl in templates.items():\\n                ctx = Context({'dt': dt, 'ICT': ICT})\\n                actual = tpl.render(ctx)\\n                expected = results[k1][k2]\\n                self.assertEqual(actual, expected, '%s / %s: %r != %r' % (k1, k2, actual, expected))\\n\\n        # Changes for USE_TZ = False\\n\\n        results['utc']['notag'] = t('utc', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict')\\n        results['ict']['notag'] = t('ict', 'eat', 'utc', 'ict')\\n\\n        with self.settings(USE_TZ=False):\\n            for k1, dt in datetimes.items():\\n                for k2, tpl in templates.items():\\n                    ctx = Context({'dt': dt, 'ICT': ICT})\\n                    actual = tpl.render(ctx)\\n                    expected = results[k1][k2]\\n                    self.assertEqual(actual, expected, '%s / %s: %r != %r' % (k1, k2, actual, expected))\\n\\n    @skipIf(zoneinfo is None, \\\"requires zoneinfo\\\")\\n    def test_localtime_filters_with_iana(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Test the |localtime, |utc, and |timezone filters with iana zones.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        # Use an IANA timezone as local time\\n        tpl = Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{{ dt|localtime }}|{{ dt|utc }}\\\")\\n        ctx = Context({'dt': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 12, 20, 30)})\\n\\n        with self.settings(TIME_ZONE='Europe/Paris'):\\n            self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"2011-09-01T12:20:30+02:00|2011-09-01T10:20:30+00:00\\\")\\n\\n        # Use an IANA timezone as argument\\n        for tz in get_timezones('Europe/Paris'):\\n            with self.subTest(repr(tz)):\\n                ctx = Context({\\n                    'dt': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30),\\n                    'tz': tz,\\n                })\\n                self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"2011-09-01T12:20:30+02:00\\\")\\n\\n    def test_localtime_templatetag_invalid_argument(self):\\n        with self.assertRaises(TemplateSyntaxError):\\n            Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{% localtime foo %}{% endlocaltime %}\\\").render()\\n\\n    def test_localtime_filters_do_not_raise_exceptions(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Test the |localtime, |utc, and |timezone filters on bad inputs.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        tpl = Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{{ dt }}|{{ dt|localtime }}|{{ dt|utc }}|{{ dt|timezone:tz }}\\\")\\n        with self.settings(USE_TZ=True):\\n            # bad datetime value\\n            ctx = Context({'dt': None, 'tz': ICT})\\n            self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"None|||\\\")\\n            ctx = Context({'dt': 'not a date', 'tz': ICT})\\n            self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"not a date|||\\\")\\n            # bad timezone value\\n            tpl = Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{{ dt|timezone:tz }}\\\")\\n            ctx = Context({'dt': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30), 'tz': None})\\n            self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"\\\")\\n            ctx = Context({'dt': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30), 'tz': 'not a tz'})\\n            self.assertEqual(tpl.render(ctx), \\\"\\\")\\n\\n    @requires_tz_support\\n    def test_timezone_templatetag(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Test the {% timezone %} templatetag.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        tpl = Template(\\n            \\\"{% load tz %}\\\"\\n            \\\"{{ dt }}|\\\"\\n            \\\"{% timezone tz1 %}\\\"\\n            \\\"{{ dt }}|\\\"\\n            \\\"{% timezone tz2 %}\\\"\\n            \\\"{{ dt }}\\\"\\n            \\\"{% endtimezone %}\\\"\\n            \\\"{% endtimezone %}\\\"\\n        )\\n        ctx = Context({\\n            'dt': datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC),\\n            'tz1': ICT,\\n            'tz2': None,\\n        })\\n        self.assertEqual(\\n            tpl.render(ctx),\\n            \\\"2011-09-01T13:20:30+03:00|2011-09-01T17:20:30+07:00|2011-09-01T13:20:30+03:00\\\"\\n        )\\n\\n    @skipIf(zoneinfo is None, \\\"requires zoneinfo\\\")\\n    def test_timezone_templatetag_with_iana(self):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Test the {% timezone %} templatetag with IANA time zone providers.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        tpl = Template(\\\"{% load tz %}{% timezone tz %}{{ dt }}{% endtimezone %}\\\")\\n\\n        # Use a IANA timezone as argument\\n        for tz in get_timezones('Europe/Paris'):\\n            with Also, receiving datetimes in UTC keeps datetime\n   680\t    arithmetic simple — there's no need for the ``normalize()`` method provided\n   681\t    by pytz.\n   682\t\n   683\t  * If you're connecting to a third-party database that stores datetimes in a\n   684\t    local time rather than UTC, then you must set this option to the\n   685\t    appropriate time zone. Also, receiving datetimes in UTC keeps datetime\\n    arithmetic simple — there's no need for the ``normalize()`` method provided\\n    by pytz.\\n\\n  * If you're connecting to a third-party database that stores datetimes in a\\n    local time rather than UTC, then you must set this option to the\\n    appropriate time zone. It can be changed at any time;\\n  the database takes care of converting datetimes to the desired time zone.\\n\\n  Setting the time zone of the database connection may be useful for running\\n  raw SQL queries involving date/time functions provided by the database, such\\n  as ``date_trunc``, because their results depend on the time zone.\\n\\n  However, this has a downside: receiving all datetimes in local time may\\n  require you to handle ambiguous or nonexistent datetimes during DST\\n  transitions using the ``fold`` attribute of :class:`datetime.datetime`\\n  objects.\\n\\n  Consider converting to local time explicitly with ``AT TIME ZONE`` in raw SQL\\n  queries instead of setting the ``TIME_ZONE`` option.\"}}"},"extras":null}}}}}

When this attribute is set and describes\n    68\tan offset, a datetime object is **aware**. This is sufficient for many use cases. In this mode, to obtain the\n    76\tcurrent time, you would write::\n    77\t\n    78\t    import datetime\n    79\t\n    80\t    now = datetime.datetime.now()\n    81\t\n    82\tWhen time zone support is enabled (:setting:`USE_TZ=True <USE_TZ>`), Django uses\n    83\ttime-zone-aware datetime objects. If your code creates datetime objects, they\n    84\tshould be aware too. In this mode, the example above becomes::\n    85\t\n    86\t    from django.utils import timezone\n    87\t\n    88\t    now = timezone.now()\n    89\t\n    90\t.. warning::\n    91\t\n    92\t    Dealing with aware datetime objects isn't always intuitive. For instance,\n    93\t    the ``tzinfo`` argument of the standard datetime constructor doesn't work\n    94\t    reliably for time zones with DST. self.assertEqual(event.dt, initial_dt + timedelta(hours=2))\n   594\t\n   595\t\n   596\t@override_settings(TIME_ZONE='Africa/Nairobi', USE_TZ=True)\n   597\tclass ForcedTimeZoneDatabaseTests(TransactionTestCase):\n   598\t    \"\"\"\n   599\t    Test the TIME_ZONE database configuration parameter.\n   600\t\n   601\t    Since this involves reading and writing to the same database through two\n   602\t    connections, this is a TransactionTestCase.\n   603\t    \"\"\"\n   604\t\n   605\t    available_apps = ['timezones']\n   606\t\n   607\t    @classmethod\n   608\t    def setUpClass(cls):\n   609\t        # @skipIfDBFeature and @skipUnlessDBFeature cannot be chained. The\n   610\t        # outermost takes precedence. Handle skipping manually instead.\n   611\t        if connection.features.supports_timezones:\n   612\t            raise SkipTest(\"Database has feature(s) supports_timezones\")\n   613\t        if not connection.features.test_db_allows_multiple_connections:\n   614\t            raise SkipTest(\"Database doesn't support feature(s): test_db_allows_multiple_connections\")\n   615\t\n   616\t        super().setUpClass()\n   617\t\n   618\t    def test_read_datetime(self):\n   619\t        fake_dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   620\t        Event.objects.create(dt=fake_dt)\n   621\t\n   622\t        with override_database_connection_timezone('Asia/Bangkok'):\n   623\t            event = Event.objects.get()\n   624\t            dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   625\t        self.assertEqual(event.dt, dt)\n   626\t\n   627\t    def test_write_datetime(self):\n   628\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 10, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   629\t        with override_database_connection_timezone('Asia/Bangkok'):\n   630\t            Event.objects.create(dt=dt)\n   631\t\n   632\t        event = Event.objects.get()\n   633\t        fake_dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, tzinfo=UTC)\n   634\t        self.assertEqual(event.dt, fake_dt)\n   635\t\n   636\t\n   637\t@override_settings(TIME_ZONE='Africa/Nairobi')\n   638\tclass SerializationTests(SimpleTestCase):\n   639\t\n   640\t    # Backend-specific notes:\n   641\t    # - JSON supports only milliseconds, microseconds will be truncated.\n   642\t    # - PyYAML dumps the UTC offset correctly for timezone-aware datetimes.\n   643\t    #   When PyYAML < 5.3 loads this representation, it subtracts the offset\n   644\t    #   and returns a naive datetime object in UTC. PyYAML 5.3+ loads timezones\n   645\t    #   correctly.\n   646\t    # Tests are adapted to take these quirks into account.\n   647\t\n   648\t    def assert_python_contains_datetime(self, objects, dt):\n   649\t        self.assertEqual(objects[0]['fields']['dt'], dt)\n   650\t\n   651\t    def assert_json_contains_datetime(self, json, dt):\n   652\t        self.assertIn('\"fields\": {\"dt\": \"%s\"}' % dt, json)\n   653\t\n   654\t    def assert_xml_contains_datetime(self, xml, dt):\n   655\t        field = parseString(xml).getElementsByTagName('field')[0]\n   656\t        self.assertXMLEqual(field.childNodes[0].wholeText, dt)\n   657\t\n   658\t    def assert_yaml_contains_datetime(self, yaml, dt):\n   659\t        # Depending on the yaml dumper, '!timestamp' might be absent\n   660\t        self.assertRegex(yaml, r\"\\n  fields: {dt: !(!timestamp)? '%s'}\" % re.escape(dt))\n   661\t\n   662\t    def test_naive_datetime(self):\n   663\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30)\n   664\t\n   665\t        data = serializers.serialize('python', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   666\t        self.assert_python_contains_datetime(data, dt)\n   667\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('python', data)).object\n   668\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   669\t\n   670\t        data = serializers.serialize('json', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   671\t        self.assert_json_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30\")\n   672\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('json', data)).object\n   673\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   674\t\n   675\t        data = serializers.serialize('xml', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   676\t        self.assert_xml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30\")\n   677\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('xml', data)).object\n   678\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   679\t\n   680\t        if not isinstance(serializers.get_serializer('yaml'), serializers.BadSerializer):\n   681\t            data = serializers.serialize('yaml', [Event(dt=dt)], default_flow_style=None)\n   682\t            self.assert_yaml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01 13:20:30\")\n   683\t            obj = next(serializers.deserialize('yaml', data)).object\n   684\t            self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   685\t\n   686\t    def test_naive_datetime_with_microsecond(self):\n   687\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 13, 20, 30, 405060)\n   688\t\n   689\t        data = serializers.serialize('python', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   690\t        self.assert_python_contains_datetime(data, dt)\n   691\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('python', data)).object\n   692\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   693\t\n   694\t        data = serializers.serialize('json', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   695\t        self.assert_json_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30.405\")\n   696\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('json', data)).object\n   697\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt.replace(microsecond=405000))\n   698\t\n   699\t        data = serializers.serialize('xml', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   700\t        self.assert_xml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T13:20:30.405060\")\n   701\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('xml', data)).object\n   702\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   703\t\n   704\t        if not isinstance(serializers.get_serializer('yaml'), serializers.BadSerializer):\n   705\t            data = serializers.serialize('yaml', [Event(dt=dt)], default_flow_style=None)\n   706\t            self.assert_yaml_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01 13:20:30.405060\")\n   707\t            obj = next(serializers.deserialize('yaml', data)).object\n   708\t            self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   709\t\n   710\t    def test_aware_datetime_with_microsecond(self):\n   711\t        dt = datetime.datetime(2011, 9, 1, 17, 20, 30, 405060, tzinfo=ICT)\n   712\t\n   713\t        data = serializers.serialize('python', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   714\t        self.assert_python_contains_datetime(data, dt)\n   715\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('python', data)).object\n   716\t        self.assertEqual(obj.dt, dt)\n   717\t\n   718\t        data = serializers.serialize('json', [Event(dt=dt)])\n   719\t        self.assert_json_contains_datetime(data, \"2011-09-01T17:20:30.405+07:00\")\n   720\t        obj = next(serializers.deserialize('json',   159\t            '    arg=[\\n'\n   160\t            '        1,\\n'\n   161\t            '        2,\\n'\n   162\t            '    ],\\n'\n   163\t            '),'\n   164\t        )\n   165\t\n   166\t    def test_nested_operation_expand_args_signature(self):\n   167\t        operation = custom_migration_operations.operations.ExpandArgsOperation(\n   168\t            arg=[\n   169\t                custom_migration_operations.operations.KwargsOperation(\n   170\t                    kwarg1=1,\n   171\t                    kwarg2=2,\n   172\t                ),\n   173\t            ]\n   174\t        )\n   175\t        buff, imports = OperationWriter(operation, indentation=0).serialize()\n   176\t        self.assertEqual(imports, {'import custom_migration_operations.operations'})\n   177\t        self.assertEqual(\n   178\t            buff,\n   179\t            'custom_migration_operations.operations.ExpandArgsOperation(\\n'\n   180\t            '    arg=[\\n'\n   181\t            '        custom_migration_operations.operations.KwargsOperation(\\n'\n   182\t            '            kwarg1=1,\\n'\n   183\t            '            kwarg2=2,\\n'\n   184\t            '        ),\\n'\n   185\t            '    ],\\n'\n   186\t            '),'\n   187\t        )\n   188\t\n   189\t\n   190\tclass WriterTests(SimpleTestCase):\n   191\t    \"\"\"\n   192\t    Tests the migration writer (makes migration files from Migration instances)\n   193\t    \"\"\"\n   194\t    class NestedEnum(enum.IntEnum):\n   195\t        A = 1\n   196\t        B = 2\n   197\t\n   198\t    class NestedChoices(models.TextChoices):\n   199\t        X = 'X', 'X value'\n   200\t        Y = 'Y', 'Y value'\n   201\t\n   202\t    def safe_exec(self, string, value=None):\n   203\t        d = {}\n   204\t        try:\n   205\t            exec(string, globals(), d)\n   206\t        except Exception as e:\n   207\t            if value:\n   208\t                self.fail(\"Could not exec %r (from value %r): %s\" % (string.strip(), value, e))\n   209\t            else:\n   210\t                self.fail(\"Could not exec %r: %s\" % (string.strip(), e))\n   211\t        return d\n   212\t\n   213\t    def serialize_round_trip(self, value):\n   214\t        string, imports = MigrationWriter.serialize(value)\n   215\t        return self.safe_exec(\"%s\\ntest_value_result = %s\" % (\"\\n\".join(imports), string), value)['test_value_result']\n   216\t\n   217\t    def assertSerializedEqual(self, value):\n   218\t        self.assertEqual(self.serialize_round_trip(value), value)\n   219\t\n   220\t    def assertSerializedResultEqual(self, value, target):\n   221\t        self.assertEqual(MigrationWriter.serialize(value), target)\n   222\t\n   223\t    def assertSerializedFieldEqual(self, value):\n   224\t        new_value = self.serialize_round_trip(value)\n   225\t        self.assertEqual(value.__class__, new_value.__class__)\n   226\t        self.assertEqual(value.max_length, new_value.max_length)\n   227\t        self.assertEqual(value.null, new_value.null)\n   228\t        self.assertEqual(value.unique, new_value.unique)\n   229\t\n   230\t    def test_serialize_numbers(self):\n   231\t        self.assertSerializedEqual(1)\n   232\t        self.assertSerializedEqual(1.2)\n   233\t        self.assertTrue(math.isinf(self.serialize_round_trip(float(\"inf\"))))\n   234\t        self.assertTrue(math.isinf(self.serialize_round_trip(float(\"-inf\"))))\n   235\t
